Fern Por-rrrcer- PRACTTcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
November 6,2006
Terry E. Dixon, City Attomey
City of Laguna Niguel
27801LaPaz Road
LagonaNiguel, CA 92677
RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No.I-06-183
Dear Mr. Dixon:
This letter is in response to your request for informal assistance regarding the
mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act').r Since yiu,luestion is
general in nafure, we_are treatingyour request as one for informal u..i.tun"".}'pl"ur"
note that nothing in this letter should be construed to evalu ate any conduct that may have
already taken place.
QUESTTONS
1' Do the Agl't prohibitions against mass mailings at public expense applyto the
LagnaNiguel chamber of commerce directo if it contains an 
I
advertisement placed by a city council member featuring the name of a city
official's business?
2. Do the A:tl: prohibitions against mass mailings at public expense apply to the
directory if it includes a listing of city council members, bus^inesses or city
council members'names as part of an alphabetical listing of all business and
individual members of the chamber?
3. Do the Act's_prohibitions against mass mailings at public expense apply if the
directory includes a roster listing of city "orn.]il members rrith th" names of
all the council members printed the same?
I Government Code sectiors 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear attitle 2,sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section g3 1 14; regulation l g3)9(c)(3), copy enclosed).


File No. I-06-183
Page No. 2
CONCLUSIONS
1. Yes- The Act's prohibitions against mass mailing at public expense apply if a
council member places an advertisement in a city-616ed chamber of
cofllmerce directory and the advertisement includes the council member,s
business name (which includes the council member's name), if it otherwise
meets the mass mailing definition in regulation 1g90r(a).
2. No. If the directory listings are not prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination or concert with the city council irembers, use of the
council members' names or names of their businisses may be permiued
provided thatthe officials are not featured in the mailing.
3 . No. The roster may be allowed under the exception in regulation 1 g90l (bX I )
and (b)(8).
FACTS
The Laguna Niguel Chamber of Commerce ("Chamber') prepares, produces and
dishibutes the Community & Business Directory (the "Directo4/) annually. The cityhas
for the last several years contributed funds to pa-y for the cost ofpiep aratioi,production,
and distribution of the Directory and the ChambLr's "Shop LagunaNiguet piogram,, (the
'?rogram'). The annual Directory is part of the Program. ThJ citybuigeted and paid to
the chamber $12,000 for the 2006Directory and the program. Th; city-council
anticipates payingthat amount to the Chamber in Decem6 er 2006 or lanuary 2007. you
understand that the costs of the Directory are paid by revenue from advertising in the
Directory ($60,000-$70,000) and the city's contribuilon, and that about one-half of the
$12,000 would be used to mail the Directory and the resi would be used for other aspects
of the Program.
The Directory includes: information about the Chamber and the City of Laguna
Niguel; the alphabetical membership listing of all members; the Business Directory
which is a listing of all business members by category of business; the governmental
listings of state and local agencies that serve raguna Niguel, and r.r*.io.r"puid display
advertisements for businesses. The Directory for a calend ax yearis mailed in January of
thatyear to all households and businesses in Laguna Niguel. Thus, well over 200 copies
would be mailed in one month.
The Chamber has business memberships, which are for individuals, corporations,
partnerships, associations or groups actively engaged in business in the ragllniNiguel
area.It also has resident memberships for individuals residing in Laguna N"iguel who


File No. I-06-183
Page No. 3
want to keep current on Chamber and community activities and to support local
businesses.
The govemmental listing includes a roster of all of the city council members,
county and state govemments, and the dates of Laguna Niguel ciiy council, commission,
and committee meetings.
A council member's name might be included in the 2007 Directory in the
following ways:
1. 
A paid display advertisement, which lists the council member,s business
and the council member's name. (This category includes companies,
which have a council member's name in the title of the company;
2. 
A paid display advertisement which lists the council member's business
and which doesn't include his name (in either the name of the business or
separately listed in the advertisement);
3. 
A council member's business and name in the alphabetical membership
listing and in the Business Directory;
4- 
A council member's business without the council member's name in the
alphabetical membership listing in the Business Directory;
5. 
A council member's name in the alphabetical membership listing for
council members who have joined as resident members and not as
business members;
6. 
A roster listing of all of the council members with the names of all the
council members printed the same.
None of these potential references to the names of council members
atd'/or their businesses would identiff them as being a council member, except for item 6.
Also the treatment of the council members' businesses andlornames in the alphabetical
membership listing and the Business Directory would be exactly the same ,. ih"
treatment of all other business and residential members of the Chu-b"..
Each of the references listed above could be included in the 2007 Directory for
one or more of the five council members. At this point, it appears that the following
references to council members would be included in the ZOOI Oirectory for each fiJting
that would not violate the prohibition on newsletters and mass mailings at public expense.
1. One council member would have apaid display advertisement. (The council
member's business and name would also be in the alphabetical membership
listing and printed in red in the Business Directory.) The proposed ad would
contain the name of the business, which includes the council member,s last
name, a brief description of the type of business, a message thanking past
Chamber presidents for their service and alist of all past presidents,-which
includes the name of the council member (as past president).


File No. I-06-183
Page No. 4
2- One council member would have his business name (which doesn,t include
his name) and his name listed in the alphabetical membership listing and in
the Business Directory.
3. Three other council members would be listed as resident members in the
alphab etical memb ership li sting.
You wish to know if any of the above listings would be considered a violation of
the prohibition on mass mailings and newsletters at public expense.
ANALYSIS
Mass Mailing Statute ond Regulation
In 1988, section 89001 of the Act was added to provide that.ho newsletter or
other mass mailing shall be sent at public expense." rnl commission adopted regulation
18901 to clarify which mailings were permissible and which were prohiUitiJunaer ttre
Act.
Under regulation 18901, a "mass mailing" is defined as more than two hundred
substantially similar tangible items delivered in a calendar month, by any means, to
recipients at their residence, place of employment, business, or post office box. (See also
section 82041.5 and regulation 18435 [defining "mass mailing,]1.)
Regulation 18901(a) provides that amailing is prohibited only if all of the
following apply
(1) Any item sent is delivered,by any means, to the recipient at his or
her residence, place of employment or business, or post office
box. For purposes of this subdivision (aXl), the item delivered to
the recipient must be a tangible item, such as a videotape, record,
button, or a written document.
(2) The item sent either:
(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing, or
(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to an
elected officer affiliated with the agencywhich produces or sends
the mailing, and is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation,
coordination, or concert with the elected officer.
(3) (A) Any of the costs of diskibution is paid for with public
moneys; or


File No. I-06-183
Page No. 5
@) cgqts of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00 are
paid with public moneys, ry! th: design, production, oiprinting is
done with the intent of sending the item oiher than u. p".-itt"a-Uy
this regulation.
(4) More than two hundred substantially similar iterns are sent, in a
single calendar month, excluding any item sent in r.rporr" to u.,
unsolicited request and any item described in subdivision (b).
Thus, a mass_mailing is prohibited if all of the factors set out in regulation
18901(a) are met. If the factors are not met, the mailing is not a ,,mass *iilirrg,, fo,
purposes of section 89001.
Delivery of a Tangible ltem/Mass Mailing
Subdivision (a)(1) of regulation 18901 only reskicts items that aremailed or
delivered, by any means, to a person's home, office, or post office box. If items are set
out for the public to pick up on their own, or are handed out in a public area, the
restrictions of the statute do not aply.
Subdivision (aX4) of regulation 18901 onlyrestricts mailings of ..substantially
similar items" tha! are "sent in a single calendar rnonth ...,, This prir,i.io, does not
include mail which is sent in response to an unsolicited request, letter or other inqui ty, or
any items that fall within the exceptions set forth in regulaiion i aeo r 6u;. (Section
8204t.4.)
Because in each of the scenarios you asked about in your letter involve more than
two hundred substantially similar tangible iterns that will be delivered in a calendar
month to recipients at their residence, pfrye of employment, businer., o, f*t office box,
the criteria in subdivisions (a)(1) and (a)(a) would be-met.
Public Expense
Regulation 18901(a)(3) specifies that amass mailing is "sent at public expense,,
*il$l the meaning of section 89001 if either: (l) anyof the costs of dishibution are
paid for with publicmoneys; or (2) more than $!6 in public money are used to pay for the
costs of design, produ_ction, and printing, and the design, production, o. prirtirg is done
with the intent of sending the item othei than us pe.miitedunder trreiegutation
Because the citypaid the Chamber $12,000 towards the distribution and
production of the Directory for fiscal year 20a6-2007 (halfof that amount was used to


File No. I-06-183
Page No. 6
mail the Directory, and the other half for other aspects of the program), this criterion is
also met.3
Thus, the Directory, which includes the various listings that you inquired about in
your letter, meets_ three criteria in regulation I 890 1 ( a)(l), (3);d 1+j rfrerefore, if the
Directory either'ofeatures an elected officer affiliat;dwiih'the agency,, or ii.rclrdes the
n€Ime, office, photograph or other reference to an elected officer afnliated with the
agency '.. and is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert
with the elected officer," it would meet all the criteria set forth under r"grriuiio,
18901(a)QXA) and (B) and would be considered a prohibited mass maiffi-
Featurtng Elected officers or References to an Elected officer
A mailing would be prohibited under regulation l}9}l(a)e) ifl (1) reference is
made to an "elected officer" affiliated with the ig"n"y which pioa*". oi sends the
mailing; or Q) the mailing includes the name, office, pho tograph,or other reference to an
elected officer affiliated with the agenly which produces or r."ar the mailing, and the
yifing is prepared or sent in cooperation, consriltation,coordination, or confert with the
electeo olilcer-
Since city council members are "elected officers," this criterion is met.a
In applying subdivision (a)Q) of regulation 18901, it must be determined which
of the two alternate tests applies. In this .,rbdivi.ior, there are two categories of mass
mailings regulated under theAct: (1) items thatfeaiure an elected officer affiliated with
the agency producing or sending the mailing , urd lZl items that do not necessarily
feature, but include, a reference to an elected officer affiliated with the ug"r"y and are
prepared or sent in cooperation with that elected officer.s
3 w'e have advi1!{-that "a mailing sent by a chamber of commerce is not ,sent at public expense,
for purposes of Section 89001 merely because the chamber receives some public moneys from a city,
unless the public entity exgressly funds the production and distribution of the mailing r;"t b;th" chamber.,,
[Emphasis added.] (see Curtis Advice r,eier,No. A-94-05:.; rrri. ir "ot 
at issue here because your facts
tLai9{e that the city has specifically allocated money for, and has expressly funded, ttr" proJrrctio, aoa
diskibution of the directory.
a Note, however, that candidates for city council who have not yet been elected to office, and are
not otherwise elected officers affiliated with the city, are not suu3ecitl th" -ur, r"uili"g i".t i"tions ofthe
Act' under the Act, an "elected- ofEcer" is any person who holds an elective office, has been elected to an
elective office but has not yet taken office, or oiho is appointed to nu a va"unt elective office. (Section
82020.)
5 Please note that these two general restrictions have some exceptions found in subdivision (b) of
regulation 18901.


File No. I-06-183
Page No. 7
If the item sent is "prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or
concert with the elected officer," then subdivision 1a;12;1B) applies. Oifrerwise, the less
restrictive standard in (a)(2)(A) applies.
Adverttsement:
You state in your letter that one council member plans to place apaid,
advertisement which, among other things, would display the name of the council
member's business, which incrudes the officials narire.-
If a mass mailing "feafures" an elected officer, it is subject to regulation 1g901.
(See regulation 18901(aX2XA).) A mass mailing "features an elected ofiicer,, if it
includes the elected officer's photograph or signature, or singles out the elected officer by
ihe-manner of display of his or her name o, offi"" in ihe layout of the document, such as
by headlines, captio-ns, !rue size, typeface, or tSpe color. (Regulation tsoolpllz); see
also regulation 1 8901 (bX9XB).)
Because the advertisement would include the council member,s name and it
yguld be displayed in amanner that would be different from other members in the
Directory, it would "feature" an elected officer and would, therefore, meet the criterion
under regulation 1 890 1 (a)(2)(A).
Additionally,by placing the advertisement in the Directory, the publication would
be "prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or in concert with,, the
council member, and thus a reference to the official's name in the advertisement and in
other listings in the Directory (including the business and membership listind; would
also meet the criterion under regulation lg90l(a)(2xB).6
Therefore, if the newsletter includes apaidadvertisement featuring an official and
it includes a reference to the official's name, all the factors set out in regulation l g90l(a)
would be met, and the mass mailing restrictions of section g9001 *ootiuppty.
Business and Membership Listings:
However, if the chamber newsletter does not include apaidadvertisement, then
the other types of listings you have inquired about would also iave to be analyzed to
determine if the last citeiaset forth in regulation rg90r(a) @(A) and (B) .,o"ra u"
satisfied.
^ 
6 
-vy'ith respect to the issue of whether or not the mere inclusion of an elected official,s name in the
name of a business is enough to bring an item within the mass mailin!provisions ofregulation
18901(a)(2), the literal language of the regulation appears to prohibit this if the.nuili"gTpr"pured or sent
in cooperation, consulation, coordination, or in concert with the official. (See Houstoiaa'vici fetter,
A-04-026.)


File No. I-06-183
Page No. 8
As stated above, "featuring" means inclusion of the elected officer,s photograph
or signature, or a singling out of the elected officer by the way his or her name or office is
displayed in the document, such as through headlines, captions, type size,typeface, or
type color.
A mailing is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert
with an elected officer if the elected offers affiliated with an agerrcyare in aly way
involved with the production or distribution of the items sent. -"This 
means, for example,
that the elected officers affiliated with the agency may not review the mailing or make
suggestions for articles to be included." (Culver Advice Letter, No. A-90-1+b.)
Your letter indicates that the council members' names may be included in the
Chamber's membership and business directory listing. You stated in your letter that
these listings would not feature any specific officials because the layout would not
display officials' ntrmes in the headline or caption. In addition, the iype size, typeface,
and color of the officials' names would be the same as all the other mlmbers tisteA in itre
membership and business directory listings. Based on the information you provided, it
does not appear that such listings would "feafure" an elected official.
Therefore, if the mailingis notprepared or sent with the cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected ofEcers, it would notbe a
prohibited mass mailing. You state that the membership and business listings ..result
from the Council Members joining the Chamber as a business or a resident and not as the
result of any additional payment or action on the part of the Council Members.,, The
officials joining of the Chamber as dues paylng members-without any additional
action-does not appear to fit the definition of elected officers actingin ,.cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert,, with the Chamber.T
Therefore, we conclude that based on the facts provided, the mass mailing
restrictions would not apply to such alphabetical business and membership listings.
Roster:
You state in your letter that the newsletter will contain a roster, or govemment
listing which will include the names of all the council members 'prinied tf,e same.,, you
do not provide any other details. Thus, based on the facts providet, the roster does not
appear to "feature" an elected officer (for instance through inclusion of the elected
officer's photographor signature, etc.) under the provisions of regulation 1g901(a)(2)(A).
As to regulation 18901(a)(2XB), the name and office of the elected officials would 
'
appear in the roster, therefore this requirement is met. Regulation l89}l(a)e)(B) also
requires that the mailing be sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, oiconcert
7 In the HoustonAdvice Letter,No. I-05-183, we advised that when an official has input or control
over the content of a mailing, the item ultimately sent "is prepared or sent in cooperation, consLrltation,
coordination, or concert with" the official. (In Houston, a city council member contolled the content of a
full page advertisement that was placed in a city-funded newiletter.)


File No. I-06-183
Page No. 9
with an elected offi"g. In the past we have advised that if the roster is approved or
authoizedby city officials (or if elected officers request the inclusion of theirname in a
roster), then the mailing would be considered sent in cooperation, consultation,
coordination, or concert with an elected officer and the officers' names may not be
included in the mass mailing, absent an express exception. (standerferAdvice Letter, No.
I-00-193; Stone Advice Letter, No. I-89-670.) Therefore, the mass mailing restrictions
would apply to such roster listings.
However, even if the mailing was sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination,
or concert with an elected officer, the roster listing may not be prohibited if it falls undei
an exception under regulation I 8901 (b).
Under the roster listing exception, a mass mailing that meets all of the
requirements under regulation 13901(a) is not prohibited if: (l) the elected officer,s nzlme
only appears in a roster listing containing the names of all ofthe elected officers of the
agency, Q) the elected officer's name appears in the same tlpe size, typeface, tlpe color,
and location as the names of all of the elected officers of the agen "y, id(3) ihe mailing-
makes no other impermissible reference to the elected officer,inctuiing a pirotograph o1
the elected officer. (Regulations 18901(b)(l) and (bXS).)
Therefore, the chamber may list the names of the council members in a roster, or
government listing, which will include the names of all the council members, if it
otherwise complies with the above requirements.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure
ERje

Because the citypaid the Chamber $12,000 towards the distribution and

mail the Directory, and the other half for other aspects of the program), this criterion is
also met.3
Thus, the Directory, which includes the various listings that you inquired about in
your letter, meets_ three criteria in regulation I 890 1 ( a)(l), (3);d 1+j rfrerefore, if the
Directory either'ofeatures an elected officer affiliat;dwiih'the agency,, or ii.rclrdes the

with the elected officer," it would meet all the criteria set forth under r"grriuiio,
18901(a)QXA) and (B) and would be considered a prohibited mass maiffi-
Featurtng Elected officers or References to an Elected officer
A mailing would be prohibited under regulation l}9}l(a)e) ifl (1) reference is
made to an "elected officer" affiliated with the ig"n"y which pioa*". oi sends the
mailing; or Q) the mailing includes the name, office, pho tograph,or other reference to an

electeo olilcer-
Since city council members are "elected officers," this criterion is met.a
In applying subdivision (a)Q) of regulation 18901, it must be determined which
of the two alternate tests applies. In this .,rbdivi.ior, there are two categories of mass
mailings regulated under theAct: (1) items thatfeaiure an elected officer affiliated with
the agency producing or sending the mailing , urd lZl items that do not necessarily
feature, but include, a reference to an elected officer affiliated with the ug"r"y and are
prepared or sent in cooperation with that elected officer.s
3 w'e have advi1!{-that "a mailing sent by a chamber of commerce is not ,sent at public expense,
for purposes of Section 89001 merely because the chamber receives some public moneys from a city,
unless the public entity exgressly funds the production and distribution of the mailing r;"t b;th" chamber.,,
[Emphasis added.] (see Curtis Advice r,eier,No. A-94-05:.; rrri. ir "ot 
at issue here because your facts
tLai9{e that the city has specifically allocated money for, and has expressly funded, ttr" proJrrctio, aoa
diskibution of the directory.
a Note, however, that candidates for city council who have not yet been elected to office, and are
not otherwise elected officers affiliated with the city, are not suu3ecitl th" -ur, r"uili"g i".t i"tions ofthe
Act' under the Act, an "elected- ofEcer" is any person who holds an elective office, has been elected to an
elective office but has not yet taken office, or oiho is appointed to nu a va"unt elective office. (Section
82020.)
5 Please note that these two general restrictions have some exceptions found in subdivision (b) of
regulation 18901.
If the item sent is "prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or
concert with the elected officer," then subdivision 1a;12;1B) applies. Oifrerwise, the less
restrictive standard in (a)(2)(A) applies.
Adverttsement:
You state in your letter that one council member plans to place apaid,
advertisement which, among other things, would display the name of the council
member's business, which incrudes the officials narire.-
If a mass mailing "feafures" an elected officer, it is subject to regulation 1g901.
(See regulation 18901(aX2XA).) A mass mailing "features an elected ofiicer,, if it
includes the elected officer's photograph or signature, or singles out the elected officer by
ihe-manner of display of his or her name o, offi"" in ihe layout of the document, such as
by headlines, captio-ns, !rue size, typeface, or tSpe color. (Regulation tsoolpllz); see
also regulation 1 8901 (bX9XB).)
Because the advertisement would include the council member,s name and it
yguld be displayed in amanner that would be different from other members in the
Directory, it would "feature" an elected officer and would, therefore, meet the criterion
under regulation 1 890 1 (a)(2)(A).
Additionally,by placing the advertisement in the Directory, the publication would
be "prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or in concert with,, the
council member, and thus a reference to the official's name in the advertisement and in
other listings in the Directory (including the business and membership listind; would
also meet the criterion under regulation lg90l(a)(2xB).6
Therefore, if the newsletter includes apaidadvertisement featuring an official and
it includes a reference to the official's name, all the factors set out in regulation l g90l(a)
would be met, and the mass mailing restrictions of section g9001 *ootiuppty.
Business and Membership Listings:
However, if the chamber newsletter does not include apaidadvertisement, then
the other types of listings you have inquired about would also iave to be analyzed to
determine if the last citeiaset forth in regulation rg90r(a) @(A) and (B) .,o"ra u"
satisfied.
^ 
6 
-vy'ith respect to the issue of whether or not the mere inclusion of an elected official,s name in the
name of a business is enough to bring an item within the mass mailin!provisions ofregulation
18901(a)(2), the literal language of the regulation appears to prohibit this if the.nuili"gTpr"pured or sent
in cooperation, consulation, coordination, or in concert with the official. (See Houstoiaa'vici fetter,
A-04-026.)
As stated above, "featuring" means inclusion of the elected officer,s photograph
or signature, or a singling out of the elected officer by the way his or her name or office is
displayed in the document, such as through headlines, captions, type size,typeface, or
type color.
A mailing is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert
with an elected officer if the elected offers affiliated with an agerrcyare in aly way
involved with the production or distribution of the items sent. -"This 
means, for example,
that the elected officers affiliated with the agency may not review the mailing or make
suggestions for articles to be included." (Culver Advice Letter, No. A-90-1+b.)
Your letter indicates that the council members' names may be included in the
Chamber's membership and business directory listing. You stated in your letter that
these listings would not feature any specific officials because the layout would not
display officials' ntrmes in the headline or caption. In addition, the iype size, typeface,
and color of the officials' names would be the same as all the other mlmbers tisteA in itre
membership and business directory listings. Based on the information you provided, it
does not appear that such listings would "feafure" an elected official.
Therefore, if the mailingis notprepared or sent with the cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected ofEcers, it would notbe a
prohibited mass mailing. You state that the membership and business listings ..result
from the Council Members joining the Chamber as a business or a resident and not as the
result of any additional payment or action on the part of the Council Members.,, The
officials joining of the Chamber as dues paylng members-without any additional
action-does not appear to fit the definition of elected officers actingin ,.cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert,, with the Chamber.T
Therefore, we conclude that based on the facts provided, the mass mailing
restrictions would not apply to such alphabetical business and membership listings.
Roster:
You state in your letter that the newsletter will contain a roster, or govemment
listing which will include the names of all the council members 'prinied tf,e same.,, you
do not provide any other details. Thus, based on the facts providet, the roster does not
appear to "feature" an elected officer (for instance through inclusion of the elected
officer's photographor signature, etc.) under the provisions of regulation 1g901(a)(2)(A).
As to regulation 18901(a)(2XB), the name and office of the elected officials would 
'
appear in the roster, therefore this requirement is met. Regulation l89}l(a)e)(B) also
requires that the mailing be sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, oiconcert
7 In the HoustonAdvice Letter,No. I-05-183, we advised that when an official has input or control
over the content of a mailing, the item ultimately sent "is prepared or sent in cooperation, consLrltation,
coordination, or concert with" the official. (In Houston, a city council member contolled the content of a
full page advertisement that was placed in a city-funded newiletter.)
with an elected offi"g. In the past we have advised that if the roster is approved or
authoizedby city officials (or if elected officers request the inclusion of theirname in a
roster), then the mailing would be considered sent in cooperation, consultation,
coordination, or concert with an elected officer and the officers' names may not be
included in the mass mailing, absent an express exception. (standerferAdvice Letter, No.
I-00-193; Stone Advice Letter, No. I-89-670.) Therefore, the mass mailing restrictions
would apply to such roster listings.
However, even if the mailing was sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination,
or concert with an elected officer, the roster listing may not be prohibited if it falls undei
an exception under regulation I 8901 (b).
Under the roster listing exception, a mass mailing that meets all of the
requirements under regulation 13901(a) is not prohibited if: (l) the elected officer,s nzlme
only appears in a roster listing containing the names of all ofthe elected officers of the
agency, Q) the elected officer's name appears in the same tlpe size, typeface, tlpe color,
and location as the names of all of the elected officers of the agen "y, id(3) ihe mailing-
makes no other impermissible reference to the elected officer,inctuiing a pirotograph o1
the elected officer. (Regulations 18901(b)(l) and (bXS).)
Therefore, the chamber may list the names of the council members in a roster, or
government listing, which will include the names of all the council members, if it
otherwise complies with the above requirements.

QUESTION: 1' Do the Agl't prohibitions against mass mailings at public expense applyto the
LagnaNiguel chamber of commerce directo if it contains an 
I
advertisement placed by a city council member featuring the name of a city
official's business?
2. Do the A:tl: prohibitions against mass mailings at public expense apply to the
directory if it includes a listing of city council members, bus^inesses or city
council members'names as part of an alphabetical listing of all business and
individual members of the chamber?
3. Do the Act's_prohibitions against mass mailings at public expense apply if the
directory includes a roster listing of city "orn.]il members rrith th" names of
all the council members printed the same?
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section g3 1 14; regulation l g3)9(c)(3), copy enclosed).

CONCLUSION: 1. Yes- The Act's prohibitions against mass mailing at public expense apply if a
council member places an advertisement in a city-616ed chamber of
cofllmerce directory and the advertisement includes the council member,s
business name (which includes the council member's name), if it otherwise
meets the mass mailing definition in regulation 1g90r(a).
2. No. If the directory listings are not prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination or concert with the city council irembers, use of the
council members' names or names of their businisses may be permiued
provided thatthe officials are not featured in the mailing.
3 . No. The roster may be allowed under the exception in regulation 1 g90l (bX I )
and (b)(8).
